skill.name: skill-developer
skill.description: Create and maintain Claude Code skills with trigger rules, hook workflows, progressive disclosure, and memory patterns. This documentation skill explains skill-rules.json, guardrail enforcement, prompt triggers, file triggers, testing commands, troubleshooting, and privacy-minded memory storage.
skill.summary: Build Claude Code skills with trigger rules, hooks, guardrails, and memory patterns.
skill.author: DojoCodingLabs

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command execution, filesystem, network, credential, and weak-crypto patterns, but review found these are Markdown documentation examples for building Claude Code skills. No executable source files, prompt injection text, credential exfiltration, or malicious network behavior were found in the reviewed skill files.

low_findings.title: Markdown Command Examples Flagged as Execution
low_findings.description: The external command findings are shell snippets that teach users how to test hooks and validate configuration. They are not executable files in this skill package, but users should review commands before copying them into a project.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed locations are fenced Markdown examples and troubleshooting commands. No script file or automatic execution path is present in the skill files.

low_findings.title: Local Hidden Directory References Are Instructional
low_findings.description: The filesystem findings point to .claude paths used for skill configuration, hook state, and memory examples. They describe normal local Claude Code project structure rather than hidden data collection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The files consistently frame these paths as local project configuration and state. The privacy section also warns not to store API keys, tokens, or credentials.

low_findings.title: Network URL Findings Are Documentation Links
low_findings.description: The network findings are references to regex101.com for testing regular expressions. They do not instruct credential transmission or background network access.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Each URL appears in user-facing documentation about regex testing. No code path sends project data to those URLs.

low_findings.title: Credential and Key Terms Are Safety Guidance
low_findings.description: Sensitive-file detections are caused by documentation warning users not to persist API keys, tokens, credentials, or private repository URLs. This is protective guidance, not credential access.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The text explicitly says not to store sensitive data and to keep memory transparent. No evidence of reading certificate files or environment secrets was found.

low_findings.title: Hook Context Injection Is Described for Guardrails
low_findings.description: The hook mechanism explains how stdout and stderr become Claude context for skill suggestions and guardrail blocks. This is legitimate for the skill domain, but copied hook output should be treated as untrusted project-controlled text.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The behavior is documented openly and matches Claude Code hook semantics. It is a real design consideration, but no malicious override text or prompt injection attempt was found.

content.value_statement: Teams need repeatable guidance when creating Claude Code skills, triggers, and guardrails. This skill provides structured documentation for rules, hooks, memory patterns, and testing workflows.
actual_capabilities: Explains how to create a SKILL.md file with frontmatter and concise guidance.
Documents skill-rules.json fields for prompt, file, content, and skip triggers.
Shows how UserPromptSubmit and PreToolUse hooks suggest or block actions.
Provides testing commands for trigger matching and hook execution checks.
Describes memory patterns for preferences, session state, and plan approval.
Includes troubleshooting steps for false positives, missing hooks, and slow patterns.
limitations: It is documentation only and does not install hooks or create skills automatically.
Examples assume a Claude Code project that already uses .claude configuration files.
Hook behavior can differ across local setups and should be tested before use.
It does not replace a security review of custom hook scripts created from the examples.

use_cases.title: Create a new domain skill
use_cases.description: Use the quick start flow to draft SKILL.md, add trigger rules, and test activation with real prompts.

use_cases.title: Design guardrail enforcement
use_cases.description: Map risky edit patterns to PreToolUse blocks, clear messages, and safe skip conditions.

use_cases.title: Debug skill activation problems
use_cases.description: Follow troubleshooting checks for keywords, regex patterns, hook registration, permissions, and performance.

prompt_templates.title: Draft a basic skill
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a new skill.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me create a Claude Code skill for [topic]. Include the SKILL.md structure, frontmatter description, and three trigger keywords.

prompt_templates.title: Add activation rules
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me add skill-rules.json entries for [skill name]. I need prompt keywords, intent patterns, file path patterns, and safe exclusions.

prompt_templates.title: Troubleshoot trigger behavior
prompt_templates.scenario: Diagnose a skill that does not activate correctly.
prompt_templates.prompt: My Claude Code skill [skill name] is not triggering for [example prompt]. Walk through keyword, regex, hook, and configuration checks.

prompt_templates.title: Design a guarded workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a guardrail skill for [risk area]. Include PreToolUse behavior, block message wording, skip conditions, testing steps, and memory boundaries.

output_examples.input: Create a skill for frontend accessibility reviews.
output_examples.output: A concise skill outline with frontmatter, activation terms, reference file suggestions, and a testing checklist.

output_examples.input: Debug why my database verification skill blocks too often.
output: Review path patterns for overly broad matches.
Check content patterns against actual edited files.
Add exclusions for tests and generated files.
Use session tracking to reduce repeated blocks.

output_examples.input: Plan memory support for a skill that learns naming preferences.
output_examples.output: A privacy-aware storage plan that keeps preferences local, adds timestamps, avoids secrets, and gives users view and clear commands.

best_practices: Keep SKILL.md concise and move detailed references into clearly named Markdown files.
Test trigger patterns against real prompts and files before publishing a skill.
Treat hook output and stored memory as sensitive project data that needs user control.
anti_patterns: Do not use broad keywords that activate a skill for unrelated work.
Do not add blocking guardrails without clear recovery steps and safe skip conditions.
Do not store credentials, tokens, personal data, or opaque memory in skill state.

faq.question: What does this skill help me build?
faq.answer: It helps you design Claude Code skills with trigger rules, hook behavior, documentation structure, and memory patterns.

faq.question: Does it create files automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and examples that you apply in your own Claude Code project.

faq.question: Can it help with guardrail skills?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains block enforcement, PreToolUse hooks, block messages, skip conditions, and testing checks.

faq.question: Does it cover prompt triggers?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers keyword triggers, intent regex patterns, file path triggers, and content patterns.

faq.question: How should I handle memory safely?
faq.answer: Store only necessary preferences, avoid secrets, include timestamps, and provide commands to view or clear memory.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The audit found documentation examples, not executable malicious code. Users should still review copied hook commands.
